target.title: Doctor: structure core maintenance repairs
target.body:
## Summary

This is PR2 in the Doctor repair stack, intended to sit after #83753.

It moves three core maintenance repairs onto the normalized health-check runner contract:

- shell completion health now exposes structured detect/repair helpers and runs through `core/doctor/shell-completion`
- systemd linger health now exposes structured detect/repair helpers and runs through `core/doctor/systemd-linger`
- startup channel maintenance now runs through `core/doctor/startup-channel-maintenance`

The legacy command helpers still work. This PR mainly extracts the same behavior into structured pieces so the runner can own lint, repair, dry-run preview, effects, warnings, and validation consistently.

## Stack Position

Depends on #83753.

Until #83753 lands, this PR includes that base contract in the GitHub diff. The incremental PR2 commit is the core maintenance repair migration only.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --cached --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-pr2-core.tsbuildinfo`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Core Doctor maintenance repairs for shell completion, systemd linger, and startup channel maintenance now return structured runner data instead of living only in ad hoc command flow.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 source checkout on branch `doctor-core-run-contract-migration`, stacked on #83753 head `1a4dc64e28f8dec1820df5aa93aa1373ec5060a5`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check`; `git diff --cached --check`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-pr2-core.tsbuildinfo`.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest proof passed for 6 files / 33 tests, then the affected command/startup subset passed again for 3 files / 13 tests. The core test-project `tsgo` pass completed successfully.

Observed result after fix: Shell completion and systemd linger checks can detect, preview, repair, and validate through the structured runner. Startup channel maintenance runs through the same repair contract and suppresses validation re-entry when called with validation scope.

What was not tested: Full platform-wide Doctor CLI scenarios and cross-OS systemd behavior were not run locally.
target.author: giodl73-repo
